zh.Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, F: 法國布格-福朗-可特艾圖希
zh.Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne, France: 法國布格-福朗-可特艾圖希
zh.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer於1076年出生在法國布格可拉望特。她於1090年與Narjot II de Toucy結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1132年在法國布格-福朗-可特艾圖希享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer于1076年出生在法国布格可拉望特。她于1090年与Narjot II de Toucy结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1132年在法国布格-福朗-可特艾圖希享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Omer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ahmed, Mohamed, Mohammed, Tarig, Mohammad, Murad, Abdirahman, Abdul, Abdulla, Abdulrahim, Ali, Amin.
Muslim: variant of   Omar  .
German: variant of   Ohmer  .

variants.name: Omar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Mohamed, Ali, Omar, Ahmed, Ahmad, Hassan, Abdul, Husam, Khalid, Mohammad, Mohd.

Muslim: from the Arabic personal name Omar of uncertain etymology, found almost exclusively among Sunni Muslims. It is thought to be related to Arabic ʿāmir ‘prosperous, full of life, large, substantial’ (see Amir 2). The root word ʿumr means ‘life’. ʿUmar bin al-Khaṭṭāb was the second of the ‘rightly guided’ caliphs (ruled 634–44); he was known by the title al-Fārūq ‘the distinguisher’, i.e. one who distinguishes between truth and falsehood. Compare Omer and Umar .
Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived by a group of elm trees, from the collective form of om ‘elm’.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Mechtild
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mechtilde
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer was born in 1076, in Cravant, Yonne, Burgundy, France. She married Narjot II de Toucy in 1090. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1132, in Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer est née en 1076, à Cravant, Yonne, Bourgogne, France. Elle a épousé Narjot II de Toucy en 1090. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1132, à Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer wurde 1076 in Cravant, Yonne, Burgund, Frankreich geboren. Sie hat 1090 Narjot II de Toucy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1132 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer è nata il nel 1076 nel Cravant, Yonne, Bourgogne, Francia. Lei sposato Narjot II de Toucy nel 1090. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1132, nel Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omerは1076年にフランスボウルゴグンイオンクラヴァントで生まれた。1090年にNarjot II de Toucyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1132年にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeイオントウシで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer(은)는 1076년 프랑스 부르고그니 욘 크라반트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1090년 Narjot II de Toucy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1132년 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 욘 토우시에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer nasceu em 1076, em Cravant, Yonne, Bourgogne, França. Ela casou-se com Narjot II de Toucy em 1090. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1132, em Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer родилась в 1076 г. в местности кравант, енн, бургогн, Франция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Narjot II de Toucy в 1090 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1132 г. в местности тоуси, енн, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Mathilde de Saint Omer nació en 1076, en Cravant, Yonne, Bourgogne, Francia. Se casó con Narjot II de Toucy en 1090. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1132, en Toucy, Yonne, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, a la edad de 56 años.
